title: exocarpos aphyllus
intro: Exocarpos aphyllus, also known as the woolly-barked or woolly-leaved native peach, is a unique shrub native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Exocarpos aphyllus are thought to include relief from arthritis, wound healing, and the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
